question: Traditional Allies/Economic Competitors
canonical_answer: Not every ally of the United States is necessarily a friend. Some relationships between governments exist as a matter of political or economic expediency where each has something that it wants or needs from the other. These countries may use methods such as industrial espionage to gain access to materials and technologies in order to gain a significant political or economic advantage.
